decision.overall_recommendation: VIX gate is open, so new entries are permissible, but this batch is a mixed bag once fundamentals are applied on top of the technical pullback pattern (which remains an unvalidated screen, not a proven edge). Six candidates (NE, HP, HUN, HE, AHCO, EYPT) were dropped for persistent EPS-miss patterns, bearish analyst skew, or negative catalysts (cyberattack disclosure, wildfire liability, M&A uncertainty) despite otherwise clean chart setups. The 18 retained names skew toward tickers with real beat streaks (GDS 8/8, GCT 7/8, SUPN 8/8, REZI/LCII/SBRA consistent beat-or-meet) and generally positive-to-mixed news flow. Position sizes are necessarily small given a ~$1,737 account and 1% risk budget (~$17.37/trade), so several picks round to only 2-4 shares — treat this as a paper/tracking exercise more than meaningful capital deployment. Sector concentration within this batch is notable (3 Technology, 3 Consumer Cyclical, 3 Industrials, 2 each in Financial Services/Basic Materials/Real Estate) — existing Webull positions (BNO, NEXT, HELP) are recorded with 'Unknown' sector tags, so true overlap with current holdings can't be confirmed and should be checked manually before sizing up. No open orders conflict with any selected ticker. Pick-tracking data is insufficient (n=4) to weigh into conviction, so sizing discipline and the earnings-buffer/stop-sanity flags below should be the primary risk guardrails.

ranked_picks.research_highlight: GDS beat EPS estimates in all 8 trailing quarters shown (e.g., $1.53 vs -$0.03 est. in the latest quarter), with revenue growth accelerating to +15.2% YoY last quarter and net income growth swinging sharply positive; AI/data-center demand is a repeated, real catalyst (Raymond James Strong Buy upgrade on AI demand, $50B yuan capex plan), though guidance has trailed estimates twice recently, pressuring the stock despite the beats.
ranked_picks.rationale: Best beat record in the shortlist plus a durable AI-demand growth story outweighs the tight-stop technical setup; guidance disappointments are a real but secondary concern.
flags: StopSanityFlag: RR 22.43 driven by a tight 1.7% stop, not an exceptional target — treat cautiously
SectorConcentration: 3 of 18 picks are Technology (REZI, GDS, GCT)
Earnings in 37 days is outside near-term buffer risk

ranked_picks.research_highlight: GigaCloud beat EPS estimates in 7 of the last 8 quarters (e.g., $1.04 vs $0.87 most recently), with an 'A' analyst rating and Buy consensus; recent news includes a furniture-market acquisition and momentum-breakout coverage, though income growth has decelerated to roughly flat in the most recent quarter after a strong multi-quarter run.
ranked_picks.rationale: Strong beat cadence, top analyst grade, and a real bolt-on acquisition support the pullback thesis; growth deceleration is the main watch item.
flags: SectorConcentration: 3 of 18 picks are Technology
Growth deceleration in most recent quarter vs prior quarters

ranked_picks.research_highlight: Supernus beat EPS estimates in all 8 trailing quarters (e.g., $0.59 vs $0.28 latest), with strong income growth; the $795M Sage Therapeutics/Zurzuvae acquisition is a real positive catalyst, offset by a negative Phase 2b trial failure (SPN-820 depression) and an earlier downgrade on slower Qelbree growth.
ranked_picks.rationale: Perfect beat streak and a completed accretive acquisition support conviction despite a real pipeline setback already priced through the stock's pullback.
flags: Small position size (4 shares) given wide $3.84 stop distance relative to risk budget

ranked_picks.research_highlight: Resideo beat or met EPS estimates in all 8 trailing quarters (e.g., $0.65 vs $0.61 latest); the Honeywell indemnity buyout was a real positive catalyst (Morgan Stanley price-target upgrade, Cramer bullish comments), though net income/EPS growth swung sharply negative most recent quarter (-72%/-82%) largely due to one-time buyout accounting noise. Analyst overall fundamentals score is weak (C-) despite the Buy consensus.
ranked_picks.rationale: Consistent beat record and a real, well-covered positive catalyst outweigh the weak DCF/ROE scores, but the recent income-growth swing warrants a smaller-conviction sizing.
flags: PriceVsPOCPct +11.68% — sits notably above its point of control, thinner volume support underneath
SectorConcentration: 3 of 18 picks are Technology

ranked_picks.research_highlight: LCI Industries beat EPS estimates in all 8 trailing quarters (e.g., $2.59 vs $2.17 latest), though quarterly revenue growth has been choppy (+16.9% then -10% then -6.4%). The Bigfoot Hydraulic Systems acquisition is a real, modest positive catalyst; analyst consensus is Hold (3 buy/9 hold/2 sell) despite the strong beat record.
ranked_picks.rationale: Rare perfect beat streak justifies a high rank even though the Hold consensus and revenue volatility temper conviction; position size is very small given the $6.61 stop distance.
flags: SectorConcentration: 3 of 18 picks are Consumer Cyclical (LCII, VFC, ADNT)
Only 2 shares fit the risk budget given wide stop distance — de minimis position

ranked_picks.research_highlight: Sabra Health Care REIT beat or met EPS estimates in 7 of 8 trailing quarters (e.g., $0.39 vs $0.1667 latest) with steadily positive revenue growth each quarter; news flow is routine (CNBC 'Final Trades' mentions) with no negative catalysts identified.
ranked_picks.rationale: Consistent, low-drama beat/meet record and steady revenue growth make this one of the more defensively reliable picks in the batch.
flags: SectorConcentration: 2 of 18 picks are Real Estate (SBRA, CTRE)

ranked_picks.research_highlight: Herbalife beat EPS estimates in 7 of the last 8 quarters (e.g., $0.64 vs $0.607 latest); the Cristiano Ronaldo 10% stake in its Pro2col platform drove a real +14% after-hours pop, a genuine positive catalyst, though earlier Cramer commentary called the stock 'too controversial, too crazy' reflecting past volatility/controversy.
ranked_picks.rationale: Strong beat cadence plus a concrete, market-moving positive catalyst support the pick despite the stock's reputational baggage.
flags: PriceVsPOCPct +5.58% — modestly above point of control, somewhat thinner support underneath

ranked_picks.research_highlight: CareTrust REIT missed narrowly in 3 of the last 4 quarters but beat in the two most recent (e.g., $0.36 vs $0.3535 latest) with strong revenue growth (+20-30% in recent quarters); analyst rating is 'A' with a strong Buy consensus (13 buy/1 strong buy vs 4 hold/1 sell) and no negative news identified.
ranked_picks.rationale: Top-tier analyst grade and strong revenue growth outweigh the recent small EPS misses, which appear to be modest guidance-timing noise rather than deterioration.
flags: SectorConcentration: 2 of 18 picks are Real Estate

ranked_picks.research_highlight: LSB Industries beat EPS estimates in 5 of the last 8 quarters (e.g., $0.27 vs $0.1296 latest) with net income growth accelerating sharply in recent quarters (+22%, +137%, +137%, +283%); news flow is routine with no major catalysts identified either way.
ranked_picks.rationale: Accelerating income growth trend is the standout positive here, even without a specific news catalyst.
flags: Earnings in 15 days — closer to the near-term window, worth confirming buffer still holds

ranked_picks.research_highlight: MDU Resources beat EPS estimates in 5 of the last 8 quarters (e.g., $0.39 vs $0.42 slight miss latest, but $0.37/$0.34/$0.32/$0.32 beats prior quarters); the newly announced $3.4B 2026-2030 infrastructure investment plan is a real positive catalyst for future rate-base growth.
ranked_picks.rationale: A credible long-term capex catalyst plus a mostly-beat record supports inclusion, though this pick has the lowest RRRatio (3.0) of the retained group.
flags: Lowest RR ratio (3.0) among selections — reward-to-risk is thinner than peers

ranked_picks.research_highlight: V.F. ranked_picks.rationale: The turnaround story has real support in the data, but the most recent quarter's sharp miss and revenue decline are a fresh red flag that tempers conviction relative to cleaner beat-streak names.

ranked_picks.research_highlight: Adient beat EPS estimates in 6 of the last 8 quarters (e.g., $0.52 vs $0.37 latest), but income growth has been extremely volatile quarter to quarter (+222%, -222%, -50%, +110%); one recent headline flagged the company 'succumbing to demand volatility,' a mild negative note with no major positive catalyst identified.
ranked_picks.rationale: Decent beat record but high income-growth volatility and the unusually large RR ratio driven by a tight stop both argue for a smaller position and lower conviction than the top picks.

ranked_picks.rationale: Improving beat trend is offset by genuine M&A uncertainty and weak underlying profitability scores, warranting a lower rank despite an attractive RR ratio.

ranked_picks.research_highlight: Fluor's beat/miss record is roughly even (4 beats, 4 misses over 8 quarters), and the most recent quarter was a large miss ($0.14 vs $0.66 est.) accompanied by a trimmed outlook — a real, recent negative catalyst ('Fluor Dips On Q1 Earnings Miss & Tweaked Outlook'); offsetting this, the company ended 2025 with a $25.5B backlog and announced a $1.4B buyback plus a new Aramco EPC deal.
ranked_picks.rationale: The most recent quarter's earnings miss and outlook cut are fresh, real negatives that outweigh the backlog/buyback positives for this ranking; the unusually high RR ratio likely reflects a tight stop.

ranked_picks.research_highlight: Seadrill missed EPS estimates in 4 of the last 5 quarters, including a large miss last quarter (-$0.68 vs $0.68 est.); a genuine positive catalyst exists ($235M in new/extended rig contracts across Malaysia, Norway and Brazil), but the earnings trend skews negative and the trade plan carries the lowest RR ratio (4.19) in the batch.
ranked_picks.rationale: Weakest recent earnings trend combined with the lowest RR ratio of any retained pick earns this the bottom rank; kept only for the concrete contract-win catalyst and technical qualification.
